key_insight: A learned control module can outperform logits-based heuristics for deciding token generation order in multimodal masked diffusion models, improving both image alignment and multimodal understanding.
engineering_relevance: For practical multimodal DLMs, generation order is a controllable design lever that can materially improve spatial fidelity in text-to-image synthesis and reasoning quality in multimodal tasks.
method: Train a control block to predict generation order instead of relying on model logits alone.
Optimize the control module with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
Apply the learned order policy to both text-to-image synthesis and multimodal understanding pipelines.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 4.08% relative improvement on GenEval for text-to-image alignment and 4.85% relative improvement on VLMEvalKit for multimodal understanding.
watch_item: The added control module and RL-based training may increase system complexity and require careful stability tuning and evaluation across broader benchmarks.
paper_title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models
